questions.question: Two angles on a straight line measure 115° and x°. What is the value of x?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. So 115° + x° = 180°. Therefore x = 180 - 115 = 65°. ✓

questions.question: Emma draws three angles around a point. Two angles are 100° and 130°. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles around a point add up to 360°. So 100° + 130° + third angle = 360°. The third angle = 360 - 100 - 130 = 130°. ✓

questions.question: A triangle has angles of 70° and 55°. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. So 70° + 55° + third angle = 180°. The third angle = 180 - 70 - 55 = 55°. ✓

questions.question: Jake measures an angle and finds it is 135°. What type of angle is this?
questions.explanation: An obtuse angle is between 90° and 180°. Since 135° is in this range, it's an obtuse angle. ✓

questions.question: An equilateral triangle has all three sides equal. What is each angle in an equilateral triangle?
questions.explanation: In an equilateral triangle, all three angles are equal. Since angles in a triangle add up to 180°, each angle = 180 ÷ 3 = 60°. ✓

questions.question: Sophie is building a rectangular frame. What is the sum of all four angles in a rectangle?
questions.explanation: All quadrilaterals (4-sided shapes) have angles that add up to 360°. A rectangle is a quadrilateral, so its angles sum to 360°. ✓

questions.question: Two angles in a triangle are 45° and 90°. What type of triangle is this?
questions.explanation: A triangle with a 90° angle is called a right-angled triangle. The third angle is 180 - 45 - 90 = 45°, making it a right-angled isosceles triangle, but 'right-angled' is the main classification. ✓

questions.question: Tom draws an isosceles triangle with two equal angles of 65° each. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, two angles are equal. The angles sum to 180°. So 65° + 65° + third angle = 180°. The third angle = 180 - 130 = 50°. ✓

questions.question: Four angles meet at a point. Three of them are 85°, 90°, and 105°. What is the fourth angle?
questions.explanation: Angles around a point add up to 360°. So 85° + 90° + 105° + fourth angle = 360°. The fourth angle = 360 - 280 = 80°. ✓

questions.question: Lucy draws a quadrilateral with angles 80°, 100°, and 95°. What is the fourth angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a quadrilateral add up to 360°. So 80° + 100° + 95° + fourth angle = 360°. The fourth angle = 360 - 275 = 85°. ✓

questions.question: An angle measures 220°. What type of angle is this?
questions.explanation: A reflex angle is between 180° and 360°. Since 220° is in this range, it's a reflex angle. ✓

questions.question: Ben measures angles on a straight line. One angle is 3x° and the other is 2x°. What is the value of x?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. So 3x + 2x = 180. This gives 5x = 180, so x = 180 ÷ 5 = 36°. ✓

questions.question: A triangle has two angles that are each x°, and a third angle of 80°. What is x?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. So x + x + 80 = 180. This gives 2x = 100, so x = 50°. ✓

questions.question: Oliver draws two parallel lines crossed by a third line. An angle on one side is 65°. What is the corresponding angle on the other parallel line?
questions.explanation: When a line crosses two parallel lines, corresponding angles are equal. So the corresponding angle is also 65°. ✓

questions.question: Mia finds that an exterior angle of a triangle is 120°. The two interior opposite angles are 70° and y°. What is y?
questions.explanation: An exterior angle of a triangle equals the sum of the two opposite interior angles. So 70° + y = 120°. Therefore y = 120 - 70 = 50°. ✓

questions.question: A regular pentagon has 5 equal sides and 5 equal angles. What is each interior angle?
questions.explanation: The sum of interior angles in a pentagon is (5-2) × 180° = 540°. In a regular pentagon, all angles are equal, so each angle = 540 ÷ 5 = 108°. ✓

questions.question: Sarah draws two angles on a straight line. One angle is twice the size of the other. What is the smaller angle?
questions.explanation: Let the smaller angle be x. Then the larger angle is 2x. On a straight line: x + 2x = 180°. So 3x = 180°, and x = 60°. ✓

questions.question: A rhombus has angles of 60°, 120°, 60°, and x°. What is x?
questions.explanation: Angles in a quadrilateral add up to 360°. So 60° + 120° + 60° + x = 360°. This gives x = 360 - 240 = 120°. ✓

questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One angle is 110°. What is the alternate angle?
questions.explanation: When parallel lines are cut by a transversal, alternate angles are equal. So the alternate angle is also 110°. ✓

questions.question: A triangle has angles of 2x°, 3x°, and 4x°. What is the value of x?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. So 2x + 3x + 4x = 180. This gives 9x = 180, so x = 20°. ✓

questions.question: Charlie draws a right-angled triangle with one other angle of 35°. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. We have 90° (right angle) + 35° + third angle = 180°. So the third angle = 180 - 90 - 35 = 55°. ✓

questions.question: A regular hexagon has 6 equal sides and 6 equal angles. What is each interior angle?
questions.explanation: The sum of interior angles in a hexagon is (6-2) × 180° = 720°. In a regular hexagon, all angles are equal, so each angle = 720 ÷ 6 = 120°. ✓

questions.question: Two angles on a straight line are 128° and x°. What is the value of x?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. So 128° + x° = 180°. Therefore x = 180 - 128 = 52°. ✓

questions.question: Emma draws a triangle with angles 48°, 67°, and y°. What is y?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. So 48° + 67° + y = 180°. Therefore y = 180 - 48 - 67 = 65°. ✓

questions.question: A triangle has angles of 95°, 42°, and z°. What is z?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. So 95° + 42° + z = 180°. Therefore z = 180 - 95 - 42 = 43°. ✓

questions.question: A quadrilateral has angles 92°, 88°, 105°, and a°. What is a?
questions.explanation: Angles in a quadrilateral add up to 360°. So 92° + 88° + 105° + a = 360°. Therefore a = 360 - 285 = 75°. ✓

questions.question: Tom draws an isosceles triangle with two equal angles of 72° each. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, two angles are equal. The angles sum to 180°. So 72° + 72° + third angle = 180°. The third angle = 180 - 144 = 36°. ✓

questions.question: Three angles meet around a point: 115°, 130°, and p°. What is p?
questions.explanation: Angles around a point add up to 360°. So 115° + 130° + p = 360°. Therefore p = 360 - 245 = 115°. ✓

questions.question: Sophie has angles on a straight line measuring 4x° and 5x°. What is x?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. So 4x + 5x = 180. This gives 9x = 180, so x = 20°. ✓

questions.question: An isosceles triangle has a base angle of 58°. What is the angle at the top?
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, the two base angles are equal. So we have 58° + 58° + top angle = 180°. The top angle = 180 - 116 = 64°. ✓

questions.question: Jake measures a right-angled triangle with one angle of 38°. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. We have 90° (right angle) + 38° + third angle = 180°. So the third angle = 180 - 90 - 38 = 52°. ✓

questions.question: A regular octagon has 8 equal sides and 8 equal angles. What is each interior angle?
questions.explanation: The sum of interior angles in an octagon is (8-2) × 180° = 1080°. In a regular octagon, all angles are equal, so each angle = 1080 ÷ 8 = 135°. ✓

questions.question: Lucy calculates that three angles around a point are 90°, 145°, and 75°. What is the fourth angle?
questions.explanation: Angles around a point add up to 360°. So 90° + 145° + 75° + fourth angle = 360°. The fourth angle = 360 - 310 = 50°. ✓

questions.question: A right-angled triangle has another angle of 61°. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. We have 90° + 61° + third angle = 180°. So the third angle = 180 - 151 = 29°. ✓

questions.question: Ben measures parallel lines crossed by a transversal. One angle is 118°. What is the corresponding angle?
questions.explanation: When a line crosses two parallel lines, corresponding angles are equal. So the corresponding angle is also 118°. ✓

questions.question: A triangle has angles measuring 3y°, 4y°, and 5y°. What is y?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. So 3y + 4y + 5y = 180. This gives 12y = 180, so y = 15°. ✓

questions.question: An exterior angle of a triangle measures 135°. Two interior opposite angles are 68° and m°. What is m?
questions.explanation: An exterior angle of a triangle equals the sum of the two opposite interior angles. So 68° + m = 135°. Therefore m = 135 - 68 = 67°. ✓

questions.question: Mia draws a quadrilateral with angles 78°, 102°, 96°, and n°. What is n?
questions.explanation: Angles in a quadrilateral add up to 360°. So 78° + 102° + 96° + n = 360°. Therefore n = 360 - 276 = 84°. ✓

questions.question: Two angles on a straight line are in the ratio 2:7. What is the smaller angle?
questions.explanation: The ratio 2:7 means the angles are 2x and 7x. On a straight line: 2x + 7x = 180°. So 9x = 180°, giving x = 20°. The smaller angle is 2x = 2 × 20 = 40°. ✓

questions.question: An isosceles triangle has two equal angles of 68° each. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle sum to 180°. So 68° + 68° + third angle = 180°. The third angle = 180 - 136 = 44°. ✓

questions.question: Charlie finds a triangle with angles 5a°, 6a°, and 7a°. What is a?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. So 5a + 6a + 7a = 180. This gives 18a = 180, so a = 10°. ✓

questions.question: Parallel lines are cut by a transversal. An angle measures 73°. What is the alternate interior angle?
questions.explanation: When parallel lines are cut by a transversal, alternate interior angles are equal. So the alternate interior angle is also 73°. ✓

questions.question: Four angles around a point are 85°, 95°, 88°, and b°. What is b?
questions.explanation: Angles around a point add up to 360°. So 85° + 95° + 88° + b = 360°. Therefore b = 360 - 268 = 92°. ✓

questions.question: Two angles on a straight line measure 7c° and 11c°. What is c?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. So 7c + 11c = 180. This gives 18c = 180, so c = 10°. ✓

questions.question: A regular heptagon has 7 equal sides and 7 equal angles. What is each interior angle? (Round to 1 decimal place)
questions.explanation: The sum of interior angles in a heptagon is (7-2) × 180° = 900°. In a regular heptagon, all angles are equal, so each angle = 900 ÷ 7 ≈ 128.6°. ✓

questions.question: An exterior angle of a triangle is 148°. The two opposite interior angles are 79° and d°. What is d?
questions.explanation: An exterior angle equals the sum of the two opposite interior angles. So 79° + d = 148°. Therefore d = 148 - 79 = 69°. ✓

questions.question: A trapezium has angles 105°, 75°, 105°, and e°. What is e?
questions.explanation: Angles in a quadrilateral add up to 360°. So 105° + 75° + 105° + e = 360°. Therefore e = 360 - 285 = 75°. ✓

questions.question: A regular nonagon has 9 equal sides and 9 equal angles. What is each interior angle?
questions.explanation: The sum of interior angles in a nonagon is (9-2) × 180° = 1260°. In a regular nonagon, all angles are equal, so each angle = 1260 ÷ 9 = 140°. ✓

questions.question: Three angles at a point measure 108°, 127°, and 84°. What is the fourth angle?
questions.explanation: Angles around a point add up to 360°. So 108° + 127° + 84° + fourth angle = 360°. The fourth angle = 360 - 319 = 41°. ✓

questions.question: An isosceles triangle has an exterior angle of 132°. The two base angles are equal. What is each base angle?
questions.explanation: The interior angle adjacent to the exterior angle is 180° - 132° = 48°. This is the top angle. In an isosceles triangle with top angle 48°, the two base angles are equal. So 48° + base + base = 180°. Each base angle = (180 - 48) ÷ 2 = 66°. ✓

questions.question: Angles on a straight line are 3f° and 2f°. What is f?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. So 3f + 2f = 180. This gives 5f = 180, so f = 36°. ✓

questions.question: A triangle has angles g°, 2g°, and 3g°. What is g?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. So g + 2g + 3g = 180. This gives 6g = 180, so g = 30°. ✓

questions.question: An isosceles triangle has two equal angles of 76° each. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle sum to 180°. So 76° + 76° + third angle = 180°. The third angle = 180 - 152 = 28°. ✓

questions.question: A regular decagon has 10 equal sides and 10 equal angles. What is each interior angle?
questions.explanation: The sum of interior angles in a decagon is (10-2) × 180° = 1440°. In a regular decagon, all angles are equal, so each angle = 1440 ÷ 10 = 144°. ✓

questions.question: A right-angled triangle has another angle of 26°. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. We have 90° + 26° + third angle = 180°. So the third angle = 180 - 116 = 64°. ✓

questions.question: A parallelogram has angles 68°, 112°, 68°, and h°. What is h?
questions.explanation: Angles in a quadrilateral add up to 360°. So 68° + 112° + 68° + h = 360°. Therefore h = 360 - 248 = 112°. ✓

questions.question: Two parallel lines are crossed by a transversal. One angle measures 124°. What is the corresponding angle?
questions.explanation: When a line crosses two parallel lines, corresponding angles are equal. So the corresponding angle is also 124°. ✓

questions.question: Two angles on a straight line are 9j° and 6j°. What is j?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. So 9j + 6j = 180. This gives 15j = 180, so j = 12°. ✓

questions.question: An isosceles triangle has a base angle of 47°. What is the top angle?
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, the two base angles are equal. So we have 47° + 47° + top angle = 180°. The top angle = 180 - 94 = 86°. ✓

questions.question: An exterior angle of a triangle is 156°. The two opposite interior angles are 88° and k°. What is k?
questions.explanation: An exterior angle equals the sum of the two opposite interior angles. So 88° + k = 156°. Therefore k = 156 - 88 = 68°. ✓

questions.question: A triangle has angles 2p°, 3p°, and 4p°. What is p?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. So 2p + 3p + 4p = 180. This gives 9p = 180, so p = 20°. ✓

questions.question: A right-angled triangle has another angle of 49°. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. We have 90° + 49° + third angle = 180°. So the third angle = 180 - 139 = 41°. ✓

questions.question: An isosceles triangle has two equal angles of 81° each. What is the third angle?

questions.question: An isosceles triangle has two equal angles of 54° each. What is the third angle?

questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. So 11c + 7c = 180. This gives 18c = 180, so c = 10°. ✓

questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. So 78° + 64° + x = 180°. Therefore x = 180 - 142 = 38°. ✓

questions.explanation: Angles in a quadrilateral add up to 360°. So 74° + 106° + 74° + y = 360°. Therefore y = 360 - 254 = 106°. ✓

questions.question: Angles on a straight line are 17z° and z°. What is z?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. So 17z + z = 180. This gives 18z = 180, so z = 10°. ✓

questions.question: An isosceles triangle has two equal angles of 70° each. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle sum to 180°. So 70° + 70° + third angle = 180°. The third angle = 180 - 140 = 40°. ✓

questions.question: An isosceles triangle has a base angle of 36°. What is the top angle?
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, the two base angles are equal. So we have 36° + 36° + top angle = 180°. The top angle = 180 - 72 = 108°. ✓

questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One angle is 93°. What is the alternate interior angle?
questions.explanation: When parallel lines are cut by a transversal, alternate interior angles are equal. So the alternate interior angle is also 93°. ✓

questions.question: A right-angled triangle has another angle of 68°. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. We have 90° + 68° + third angle = 180°. So the third angle = 180 - 158 = 22°. ✓

questions.question: Four angles around a point are 96°, 109°, 88°, and b°. What is b?
questions.explanation: Angles around a point add up to 360°. So 96° + 109° + 88° + b = 360°. Therefore b = 360 - 293 = 67°. ✓

questions.question: Two angles on a straight line are in the ratio 5:13. What is the smaller angle?
questions.explanation: The ratio 5:13 means the angles are 5x and 13x. On a straight line: 5x + 13x = 180°. So 18x = 180°, giving x = 10°. The smaller angle is 5x = 5 × 10 = 50°. ✓

questions.question: What do angles on a straight line add up to?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line always add up to 180°. This is one of the key angle facts to remember. ✓

questions.question: Two angles are on a straight line. One angle is 115°. What is the other angle?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. The other angle = 180° − 115° = 65°. ✓

questions.question: Two angles sit on a straight line. One angle is 72°. What is the other angle?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. The other angle = 180° − 72° = 108°. ✓

questions.question: An angle of 90° sits on a straight line with another angle. What is the other angle?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. The other angle = 180° − 90° = 90°. Both angles are right angles. ✓

questions.question: Two angles are formed on a straight line. One is 143°. What is the other?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. The other angle = 180° − 143° = 37°. ✓

questions.question: Oliver draws a straight line and marks an angle of 58° on one side. What is the angle on the other side?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. The other angle = 180° − 58° = 122°. ✓

questions.question: Emma measures one of two angles on a straight line and gets 165°. What is the other angle?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. The other angle = 180° − 165° = 15°. ✓

questions.question: Two angles on a straight line are equal. What is each angle?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. If both are equal, each angle = 180° ÷ 2 = 90°. ✓

questions.question: Three angles on a straight line are 54°, 68°, and x°. What is x?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. So 54° + 68° + x° = 180°. That gives 122° + x° = 180°, so x = 180 − 122 = 58°. ✓

questions.question: Three angles sit on a straight line. Two of them are 37° and 85°. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. The third angle = 180° − 37° − 85° = 58°. ✓

questions.question: Two straight lines cross each other. One of the angles formed is 125°. What is the angle vertically opposite to it?
questions.explanation: Vertically opposite angles are always equal. So the angle opposite 125° is also 125°. ✓

questions.question: Two straight lines cross. One angle is 70°. What is the angle next to it on the straight line?
questions.explanation: Adjacent angles on a straight line add up to 180°. The angle next to 70° = 180° − 70° = 110°. ✓

questions.question: Sophie marks three angles on a straight line: 42°, y°, and 73°. What is y?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. So 42° + y° + 73° = 180°. That gives 115° + y° = 180°, so y = 180 − 115 = 65°. ✓

questions.question: Two angles on a straight line are in the ratio 1:3. What is the larger angle?
questions.explanation: The angles are in the ratio 1:3, so they are 1 part and 3 parts. Total parts = 4. Each part = 180° ÷ 4 = 45°. The larger angle = 3 × 45° = 135°. ✓

questions.question: Jack draws two angles on a straight line. One angle is twice the size of the other. What is the smaller angle?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. If one is twice the other, call them x° and 2x°. So x + 2x = 180, meaning 3x = 180, so x = 60°. The smaller angle is 60°. ✓

questions.question: Three equal angles sit on a straight line. What is each angle?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. If all three are equal, each angle = 180° ÷ 3 = 60°. ✓

questions.question: Four angles on a straight line are 28°, 47°, 63°, and w°. What is w?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. So 28° + 47° + 63° + w° = 180°. That gives 138° + w° = 180°, so w = 180 − 138 = 42°. ✓

questions.question: A door is open, making an angle of 65° with the wall. What is the angle between the door and the wall on the other side?
questions.explanation: The door and the wall form a straight line (180°). The angle on the other side = 180° − 65° = 115°. ✓

questions.question: Angle a is on a straight line with 130°. Angle b is vertically opposite to angle a. What is b?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. So angle a = 180° − 130° = 50°. Vertically opposite angles are equal, so angle b = 50°. ✓

questions.question: Four angles on a straight line are 15°, 35°, x°, and 55°. What is x?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. So 15° + 35° + x° + 55° = 180°. That gives 105° + x° = 180°, so x = 180 − 105 = 75°. ✓

questions.question: Lily has two angles on a straight line. One angle is 3 times the size of the other. What is the smaller angle?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. Call the smaller angle x°. The larger is 3x°. So x + 3x = 180, meaning 4x = 180, so x = 45°. The smaller angle is 45°. ✓

questions.question: Angle p and angle q are on a straight line. Angle r is vertically opposite to angle q. If p = 68°, what is angle r?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. So angle q = 180° − 68° = 112°. Vertically opposite angles are equal, so angle r = 112°. ✓

questions.question: An angle measures 47°. What type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: An acute angle is less than 90°. Since 47° is less than 90°, it is an acute angle. ✓

questions.question: An angle measures 135°. What type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: An obtuse angle is between 90° and 180°. Since 135° is between 90° and 180°, it is an obtuse angle. ✓

questions.question: Which of these is a right angle?
questions.explanation: A right angle is exactly 90°. ✓

questions.question: Sophie measures an angle of 180°. What type of angle is this?
questions.explanation: An angle of exactly 180° is called a straight angle. It forms a straight line. ✓

questions.question: An angle measures 82°. What type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: An acute angle is less than 90°. Since 82° is less than 90°, it is an acute angle. ✓

questions.question: Harry measures an angle of 163°. What type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: An obtuse angle is between 90° and 180°. Since 163° is between 90° and 180°, it is an obtuse angle. ✓

questions.question: An angle is greater than 180° but less than 360°. What type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: A reflex angle is any angle greater than 180° but less than 360°. ✓

questions.question: Lily says an angle of 200° is obtuse. Is she correct?
options: Yes, because it is bigger than 90°
Yes, because it is bigger than 180°
No, it is a reflex angle
No, it is a straight angle
No, it is an acute angle
questions.correctAnswer: No, it is a reflex angle
questions.explanation: An obtuse angle is between 90° and 180°. An angle of 200° is greater than 180°, so it is a reflex angle, not obtuse. Lily is incorrect. ✓

questions.question: Oliver draws a triangle. Two of its angles are 50° and 60°. What type of angle is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. The third angle = 180° − 50° − 60° = 70°. Since 70° is less than 90°, it is an acute angle. ✓

questions.question: Which of these angles is reflex?
questions.explanation: A reflex angle is greater than 180° and less than 360°. Only 181° is in that range, so 181° is a reflex angle. ✓

questions.question: Jack draws a triangle with angles 25° and 35°. What type of angle is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. The third angle = 180° − 25° − 35° = 120°. Since 120° is between 90° and 180°, it is an obtuse angle. ✓

questions.question: A clock shows 4 o'clock. What type of angle is formed between the hour hand and the minute hand?
questions.explanation: Each hour on a clock represents 30° (since 360° ÷ 12 = 30°). At 4 o'clock, the hands are 4 hours apart, so the angle = 4 × 30° = 120°. Since 120° is between 90° and 180°, it is an obtuse angle. ✓

questions.question: The minute hand of a clock turns from 12 to 9. How many degrees does it turn through, and what type of angle is this?
questions.explanation: From 12 to 9, the minute hand passes through 9 hours on the clock face. Each hour = 30° (360° ÷ 12 = 30°). So the turn = 9 × 30° = 270°. Since 270° is between 180° and 360°, it is a reflex angle. ✓

questions.question: An obtuse angle is exactly 3 times the size of a 40° angle. What is the obtuse angle?
questions.explanation: 3 × 40° = 120°. Since 120° is between 90° and 180°, it is indeed an obtuse angle. ✓

questions.question: A triangle has angles of 60° and 80°. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. So 60° + 80° + third angle = 180°. The third angle = 180 - 60 - 80 = 40°. ✓

questions.question: A triangle has angles of 50° and 90°. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. So 50° + 90° + third angle = 180°. The third angle = 180 - 50 - 90 = 40°. ✓

questions.question: A triangle has angles of 75° and 65°. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. So 75° + 65° + third angle = 180°. The third angle = 180 - 75 - 65 = 40°. ✓

questions.question: A triangle has angles of 45° and 85°. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. So 45° + 85° + third angle = 180°. The third angle = 180 - 45 - 85 = 50°. ✓

questions.question: A triangle has angles of 38° and 72°. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. So 38° + 72° + third angle = 180°. The third angle = 180 - 38 - 72 = 70°. ✓

questions.question: Emma draws a triangle with angles 35° and 110°. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. So 35° + 110° + third angle = 180°. The third angle = 180 - 35 - 110 = 35°. This is an obtuse triangle because one angle is greater than 90°. ✓

questions.question: Oliver draws a triangle. One angle is 28° and another is 124°. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. So 28° + 124° + third angle = 180°. The third angle = 180 - 28 - 124 = 28°. This is an obtuse triangle with two equal angles, making it an obtuse isosceles triangle. ✓

questions.question: Lily measures two angles in a triangle as 56° and 97°. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. So 56° + 97° + third angle = 180°. The third angle = 180 - 56 - 97 = 27°. ✓

questions.question: Sophie draws a triangle where one angle is twice another. The two angles are 46° and 92°. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. So 46° + 92° + third angle = 180°. The third angle = 180 - 46 - 92 = 42°. ✓

questions.question: Jack draws a triangle with angles of 15° and 135°. What type of triangle is this, and what is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. The third angle = 180 - 15 - 135 = 30°. Since one angle (135°) is greater than 90°, this is an obtuse triangle. ✓

questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A is twice angle B. Angle C is 36°. What is angle A?
questions.explanation: Let angle B = x. Then angle A = 2x. We know x + 2x + 36 = 180. So 3x = 144, giving x = 48°. Angle A = 2 × 48 = 96°. ✓

questions.question: In triangle PQR, angle P = angle Q. Angle R is 50°. What is angle P?
questions.explanation: Since angle P = angle Q, let each be x. Then x + x + 50 = 180. So 2x = 130, giving x = 65°. Angle P = 65°. ✓

questions.question: In a triangle, one angle is three times the smallest angle, and the third angle is 60°. The smallest angle is x°. What is the largest angle?
questions.explanation: Let the smallest angle be x. The other angle is 3x. So x + 3x + 60 = 180. This gives 4x = 120, so x = 30°. The largest angle = 3 × 30 = 90°. But we must check: 60° is also an angle. Since 90° > 60° > 30°, the largest angle is 90°. ✓

questions.question: In a triangle, two angles are equal and the third angle is 30° more than each of the equal angles. What are the equal angles?
questions.explanation: Let each equal angle be x. The third angle is x + 30. So x + x + (x + 30) = 180. This gives 3x + 30 = 180, so 3x = 150 and x = 50°. Each equal angle is 50°. ✓

questions.question: Four angles meet at a point. Three of them are 78°, 112°, and 95°. What is the fourth angle?
questions.explanation: Angles around a point add up to 360°. So 78° + 112° + 95° + fourth angle = 360°. The fourth angle = 360 - 285 = 75°. ✓

questions.question: Three angles meet at a point. Two of them are 148° and 97°. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles around a point add up to 360°. So 148° + 97° + third angle = 360°. The third angle = 360 - 245 = 115°. ✓

questions.question: Five angles meet at a point. Four of them are 65°, 82°, 74°, and 91°. What is the fifth angle?
questions.explanation: Angles around a point add up to 360°. So 65° + 82° + 74° + 91° + fifth angle = 360°. The fifth angle = 360 - 312 = 48°. ✓

questions.question: Four angles around a point are 118°, 76°, 83°, and x°. What is x?
questions.explanation: Angles around a point add up to 360°. So 118° + 76° + 83° + x = 360°. Therefore x = 360 - 277 = 83°. ✓

questions.question: Three roads meet at a roundabout. The angles between them are 156°, 98°, and y°. What is y?
questions.explanation: Angles around a point add up to 360°. So 156° + 98° + y = 360°. Therefore y = 360 - 254 = 106°. ✓

questions.question: Four angles meet at a point. Three of them are 64°, 128°, and 96°. What is the fourth angle?
questions.explanation: Angles around a point add up to 360°. So 64° + 128° + 96° + fourth angle = 360°. The fourth angle = 360 - 288 = 72°. ✓

questions.question: Three equal angles and one angle of 90° meet at a point. What is each equal angle?
questions.explanation: Let each equal angle be x. Then x + x + x + 90 = 360. So 3x = 270, giving x = 90°. Each equal angle is 90°. ✓

questions.question: The minute hand of a clock points at 12. It turns clockwise to point at 8. What angle has it turned through?
questions.explanation: The clock face is divided into 12 equal sections, each covering 360° ÷ 12 = 30°. From 12 to 8 is 8 sections. So the angle = 8 × 30° = 240°. ✓

questions.question: Two angles around a point are equal, and two other angles are 70° and 130°. What is each equal angle?
questions.explanation: Let each equal angle be x. Then x + x + 70 + 130 = 360. So 2x + 200 = 360, giving 2x = 160 and x = 80°. Each equal angle is 80°. ✓

questions.question: The minute hand of a clock points at 12. It turns clockwise to point at 5. What angle has it turned through?
questions.explanation: Each number on the clock face is 360° ÷ 12 = 30° apart. From 12 to 5 is 5 sections. So the angle = 5 × 30° = 150°. ✓

questions.question: Four equal angles meet at a point, but one of them is then split into two parts: 50° and the rest. What is 'the rest'?
questions.explanation: If four equal angles meet at a point, each is 360° ÷ 4 = 90°. One angle is split into 50° and the rest. So the rest = 90 - 50 = 40°. ✓

questions.question: An irregular quadrilateral has angles of 115°, 82°, and 74°. What is the fourth angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a quadrilateral add up to 360°. So 115° + 82° + 74° + fourth angle = 360°. The fourth angle = 360 - 271 = 89°. ✓

questions.question: A parallelogram has one angle of 65°. What is the angle directly opposite to it?
questions.explanation: In a parallelogram, opposite angles are equal. So the angle opposite 65° is also 65°. ✓

questions.question: A parallelogram has one angle of 72°. What is the angle next to it?
questions.explanation: In a parallelogram, adjacent angles add up to 180° (they are co-interior angles). So the angle next to 72° = 180 - 72 = 108°. ✓

questions.question: A quadrilateral has angles of 93°, 107°, and 68°. What is the fourth angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a quadrilateral add up to 360°. So 93° + 107° + 68° + fourth angle = 360°. The fourth angle = 360 - 268 = 92°. ✓

questions.question: A trapezium has two parallel sides. One angle is 58°. The angle on the same side (co-interior) must add up to 180°. What is that co-interior angle?
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles between parallel lines add up to 180°. So the co-interior angle = 180 - 58 = 122°. ✓

questions.question: Three angles of a quadrilateral are 80°, 100°, and 95°. What is the fourth angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a quadrilateral add up to 360°. So 80° + 100° + 95° + fourth angle = 360°. The fourth angle = 360 - 275 = 85°. ✓

questions.question: A kite has two pairs of equal adjacent angles. One pair of equal angles is 110° each. What is each angle in the other pair?
questions.explanation: Angles in a quadrilateral add up to 360°. The kite has two angles of 110°. So 110° + 110° + x + x = 360°. This gives 2x = 140, so x = 70°. Each angle in the other pair is 70°. ✓

questions.question: A pentagon has angles of 108°, 112°, 95°, 120° and x°. What is x?
questions.explanation: The sum of interior angles in a pentagon is (5−2) × 180° = 540°. So 108° + 112° + 95° + 120° + x = 540°. The four known angles add up to 435°. Therefore x = 540 − 435 = 105°. ✓

questions.question: Grace draws a pentagon with angles 98°, 124°, 106°, 110° and y°. What is y?
questions.explanation: The sum of interior angles in a pentagon is (5−2) × 180° = 540°. So 98° + 124° + 106° + 110° + y = 540°. The four known angles add up to 438°. Therefore y = 540 − 438 = 102°. ✓

questions.question: A hexagon has angles of 130°, 110°, 125°, 115°, 140° and z°. What is z?
questions.explanation: The sum of interior angles in a hexagon is (6−2) × 180° = 720°. So 130° + 110° + 125° + 115° + 140° + z = 720°. The five known angles add up to 620°. Therefore z = 720 − 620 = 100°. ✓

questions.question: A hexagon has angles of 100°, 135°, 120°, 115°, 130° and a°. What is a?
questions.explanation: The sum of interior angles in a hexagon is (6−2) × 180° = 720°. So 100° + 135° + 120° + 115° + 130° + a = 720°. The five known angles add up to 600°. Therefore a = 720 − 600 = 120°. ✓

questions.question: A pentagon has angles 100°, 110°, 120°, 130° and x°. Find x.
questions.explanation: The sum of interior angles in a pentagon is (5−2) × 180° = 540°. So 100° + 110° + 120° + 130° + x = 540°. The four known angles add up to 460°. Therefore x = 540 − 460 = 80°. ✓

questions.question: A heptagon (7-sided shape) has six angles of 115°, 130°, 140°, 125°, 135° and 120°. What is the seventh angle?
questions.explanation: The sum of interior angles in a heptagon is (7−2) × 180° = 900°. The six given angles add up to 115° + 130° + 140° + 125° + 135° + 120° = 765°. So the seventh angle = 900° − 765° = 135°. ✓

questions.question: Oliver draws a heptagon (7 sides). Six of the angles are 128°, 132°, 119°, 145°, 126° and 110°. What is the seventh angle?
questions.explanation: The sum of interior angles in a heptagon is (7−2) × 180° = 900°. The six given angles add up to 128° + 132° + 119° + 145° + 126° + 110° = 760°. So the seventh angle = 900° − 760° = 140°. ✓

questions.question: A pentagon has three angles of 108° each. The other two angles are equal. What is each of the equal angles?
questions.explanation: The sum of interior angles in a pentagon is (5−2) × 180° = 540°. Three angles of 108° give 3 × 108° = 324°. The remaining two equal angles share 540° − 324° = 216°. Each one = 216° ÷ 2 = 108°. ✓

questions.question: What is each interior angle of a regular hexagon?
questions.explanation: The sum of interior angles in a hexagon is (6−2) × 180° = 720°. A regular hexagon has 6 equal angles, so each angle = 720° ÷ 6 = 120°. ✓

questions.question: How many degrees is each interior angle of a regular pentagon?
questions.explanation: The sum of interior angles in a pentagon is (5−2) × 180° = 540°. A regular pentagon has 5 equal angles, so each angle = 540° ÷ 5 = 108°. ✓

questions.question: What is the sum of the interior angles in any triangle?
questions.explanation: The interior angles in any triangle always add up to 180°. This is one of the most important angle facts to remember. ✓

questions.question: What is the sum of the interior angles in any quadrilateral?
questions.explanation: A quadrilateral has 4 sides. The sum of interior angles = (4−2) × 180° = 2 × 180° = 360°. ✓

questions.question: A regular polygon has interior angles of 120° each. How many sides does it have?
questions.explanation: For a regular polygon, each interior angle = ((n−2) × 180°) ÷ n. Setting this equal to 120° gives (n−2) × 180 = 120n. So 180n − 360 = 120n, meaning 60n = 360 and n = 6. It has 6 sides — it's a hexagon. ✓

questions.question: What is the sum of the interior angles of a hexagon?
questions.explanation: The sum of interior angles in any polygon is (n−2) × 180°. For a hexagon, n = 6, so the sum = (6−2) × 180° = 4 × 180° = 720°. ✓

questions.question: A right-angled triangle sits on a straight line. The right angle is at the bottom-left corner. The angle at the top of the triangle is 35°. What is the angle between the sloping side and the straight line, outside the triangle?
questions.explanation: The triangle's angles are 90°, 35° and 180° − 90° − 35° = 55°. The bottom-right angle inside the triangle is 55°. The angle between the sloping side and the straight line outside the triangle = 180° − 55° = 125°. ✓

questions.question: In a right-angled triangle, one of the other angles is 28°. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. We have 90° + 28° + third angle = 180°. The third angle = 180° − 90° − 28° = 62°. ✓

questions.question: Sophie draws a right-angled triangle on a straight line. The angle at the top of the triangle is 50°. What is the angle between the sloping side of the triangle and the straight line, outside the triangle?
questions.explanation: The triangle's angles are 90°, 50° and 180° − 90° − 50° = 40°. The base angle next to the straight line is 40°. The angle outside the triangle on the straight line = 180° − 40° = 140°. ✓

questions.question: In a right-angled triangle, one angle is twice the size of the other non-right angle. What are the two smaller angles?
questions.explanation: Let the smaller angle be x°. The other non-right angle is 2x°. So 90° + x + 2x = 180°. This gives 3x = 90°, so x = 30°. The two angles are 30° and 60°. ✓

questions.question: In a right-angled triangle, one angle is three times the size of the other non-right angle. What is the smallest angle in the triangle?
questions.explanation: Let the smaller non-right angle be x°. The other is 3x°. So 90° + x + 3x = 180°. This gives 4x = 90°, so x = 22.5°. The smallest angle is 22.5°. ✓

questions.question: An isosceles triangle has two equal angles of 70°. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, two angles are equal. Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. So 70° + 70° + third angle = 180°. The third angle = 180° − 140° = 40°. ✓

questions.question: An isosceles triangle has two equal angles of 50°. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, two angles are equal. Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. So 50° + 50° + third angle = 180°. The third angle = 180° − 100° = 80°. ✓

questions.question: An isosceles triangle has two equal angles of 55°. What is the third angle?
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, two angles are equal. Angles in a triangle add up to 180°. So 55° + 55° + third angle = 180°. The third angle = 180° − 110° = 70°. ✓

questions.question: The interior angle of a triangle at one vertex is 60°. What is the exterior angle at that vertex?
questions.explanation: An interior angle and its exterior angle sit on a straight line, so they add up to 180°. The exterior angle = 180° − 60° = 120°. ✓

questions.question: The interior angle of a triangle at one vertex is 75°. What is the exterior angle at that vertex?
questions.explanation: An interior angle and its exterior angle sit on a straight line, so they add up to 180°. The exterior angle = 180° − 75° = 105°. ✓

questions.question: The exterior angle of a triangle is 110°. The two non-adjacent interior angles are equal. What is each one?
questions.explanation: An exterior angle of a triangle equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles. So the two equal angles add up to 110°. Each angle = 110° ÷ 2 = 55°. ✓

questions.question: The exterior angle of a triangle is 124°. The two non-adjacent interior angles are equal. What is each one?
questions.explanation: An exterior angle of a triangle equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles. So the two equal angles add up to 124°. Each angle = 124° ÷ 2 = 62°. ✓

questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One angle is 70°. What is the corresponding angle?
questions.explanation: When a transversal crosses two parallel lines, corresponding angles are equal. So the corresponding angle is also 70°. ✓

questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One angle is 115°. What is the alternate angle?
questions.explanation: When a transversal crosses two parallel lines, alternate angles are equal. So the alternate angle is also 115°. ✓

questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One angle is 48°. What is the corresponding angle on the other parallel line?
questions.explanation: When a transversal crosses two parallel lines, corresponding angles are equal. So the corresponding angle is also 48°. ✓

questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One angle is 55°. Co-interior angles (same side of the transversal, between the parallel lines) add up to 180°. What is the co-interior angle?
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles (also called allied angles) add up to 180°. So the co-interior angle = 180° − 55° = 125°. ✓

questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One angle is 68°. What is the co-interior angle on the same side?
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles add up to 180°. So the co-interior angle = 180° − 68° = 112°. ✓

questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal forming an angle of 72°. What are the two different angle sizes formed at each intersection?
questions.explanation: At each intersection, adjacent angles on a straight line add up to 180°. So the two angle sizes are 72° and 180° − 72° = 108°. ✓

questions.question: Two angles on a straight line are x° and 130°. What is x?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. So x + 130 = 180. Therefore x = 180 − 130 = 50°. ✓

questions.question: Two angles on a straight line are x° and 95°. What is x?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°. So x + 95 = 180. Therefore x = 180 − 95 = 85°. ✓

questions.question: Angles around a point are x°, 100° and 150°. What is x?
questions.explanation: Angles around a point add up to 360°. So x + 100 + 150 = 360. Therefore x = 360 − 250 = 110°. ✓
